unit_22 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_22

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 413 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　the seminal; concedes; original
conceptionperceptiveproject
conception: standard unit headword | perceptive: standard unit headword | project: standard unit headword
#2 SE
Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent
pugnaciousrealize
pugnacious: standard unit headword | realize: standard unit headword

答案：C　comity

#4 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
yieldmammal
yield: standard unit headword | mammal: standard unit headword
#5 SE
For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.
答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility

答案：E　a vacuous pastime

A routine is sometimes conceived as constraining—a self-made prison of inflexible habits that prevent people from changing their lives. But routine is also seen as (ii) : activities that become routine (iii) ; allowing us to expend energy on other things.

Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　polarity; liberating; demand less attention

答案：A, F　short-lived; ephemeral

答案：C, D　confused; muddled

答案：A　ancient lineages

答案：A, B　abound; proliferate

#50 TC TRIPLE
Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　confound; puncture; facile
demolish
demolish: standard unit headword
#51 TC SINGLE
Few studies have been published on ground-squirrel dispersal, and most of them have involved very small sample sizes, thus most statement regarding ground-squirrel dispersal must be considered ______.Few studies have been published on ground-squirrel dispersal, and most of them have involved very small sample sizes, thus most statement regarding ground-squirrel dispersal must be considered ______.
答案：E　conjectural
invaluableroutine
invaluable: standard unit headword | routine: standard unit headword
#52 TC DOUBLE
Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　swagger; pugnacious
pugnaciousshoulder
pugnacious: standard unit headword | shoulder: standard unit headword
#53 SE
Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less ______ texts.Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less ______ texts.
答案：A, F　exhaustive; comprehensive
mien
mien: standard unit headword

However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

replicate: standard unit headword

答案：C　egalitarian

These musicians use computers to (ii) and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. These musicians use computers to (ii) and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii) these developments.

Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　admirable; unusual

The brain has become, for many people, ______ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.The brain has become, for many people, ______ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.
答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with

答案：B　bemoaned

#60 TC DOUBLE
The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)_______, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)_______.The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)_______, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism

averse: standard unit headword
#61 TC TRIPLE
Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)______ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)______. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)______ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)______ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)______. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)______ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of
well-being
well-being: standard unit headword
#62 SE
Artist Uzo Egonu's transition from a cultural agenda to a political one was less premeditated than it was ____________, imposed upon him by the political turmoil, in the late 1960s, in his native Nigeria.Artist Uzo Egonu's transition from a cultural agenda to a political one was less premeditated than it was ____________, imposed upon him by the political turmoil, in the late 1960s, in his native Nigeria.
答案：A, C　exigent; imperative
imperative
imperative: standard unit headword

答案：C　specious

A (i)______ of this method is that its predictions (ii)______ actual observations, but the method also makes the questionable assumptions that the future will resemble the present.

Blank 2

#65 TC TRIPLE
Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________ even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________ even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging
relentless
relentless: standard unit headword

The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to __________ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to __________ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.
答案：A, D　trump; supplant

Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. [fMSARS: 5kREIIGRE]Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. [fMSARS: 5kREIIGRE]

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for

答案：A, F　unbridled ambition; solipsistic

Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort togain___; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort togain___; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.
答案：C　prestige

It is part of (ii)______ in the museum’s architecture and design department, which in the past has championed architecture’s artistic value over its real-world consequences.The museum’s compelling new architectural exhibition looks at eleven projects around the world that have had major (i)______ impacts despite modest budget. It is part of (ii)______ in the museum’s architecture and design department, which in the past has championed architecture’s artistic value over its real-world consequences.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　social; a shift in philosophy

Harper Lee’s narration in To kill a Mocking Bird is ______, mixing an adult’s and a child’s perspective according to no logic other than the immediate exigencies of the plot.Harper Lee’s narration in To kill a Mocking Bird is ______, mixing an adult’s and a child’s perspective according to no logic other than the immediate exigencies of the plot.
答案：B, E　a hodgepodge; a patchwork

In the discussions on international patent law, many (i)______ issues will probably be pushed far into the future. This cautious approach makes diplomatic sense, since attempts over the past 15 years to reach international agreements on patents have (ii)______ just such sensitive issues.In the discussions on international patent law, many (i)______ issues will probably be pushed far into the future. This cautious approach makes diplomatic sense, since attempts over the past 15 years to reach international agreements on patents have (ii)______ just such sensitive issues.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　contentious; foundered on

#104 TC SINGLE
A curiosity of the film Vertigo is its capacity to generate emotional power from a plot that lacks the most of elementary ______: viewers are required to accept not an isolated implausibility, but a continuous stream of them.A curiosity of the film Vertigo is its capacity to generate emotional power from a plot that lacks the most of elementary ______: viewers are required to accept not an isolated implausibility, but a continuous stream of them.
答案：A　believability

#105 SE
Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ ______ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ ______ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.
答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic

generate: standard unit headword
#106 TC TRIPLE
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data
yield
yield: standard unit headword

答案：B　sanguine

Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)______ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)______ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)______ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. Therefore, lying (iii)______ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

#111 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　demurred; assuage the qualms

答案：C, F　a nebulous; a vague

答案：C　contradicted

It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrate on the work of either the nineteenth century pioneers or the twentieth century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. 答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive

答案：D　immutable

答案：B, D　speculative; conjectural

But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B, D　forsaken; conventional
replicate
replicate: standard unit headword
#201 TC TRIPLE
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence
project
project: standard unit headword
#202 TC TRIPLE
The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i) , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii) act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii) in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly. [#The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i) , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii) act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii) in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly. [#
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift

Blank 2
答案：A, E　intransigent; uncompromising

However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)_____ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.

答案：C, F　cumulative; additive

#219 TC TRIPLE
The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring

To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. #290 TC TRIPLE

In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) ___________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) ___________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) ___________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) ___________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) ___________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　originality; avant-garde; abandon
obsolete
obsolete: standard unit headword
#291 TC TRIPLE
Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　affords; getting take-out food; former
nutrient
nutrient: standard unit headword
#292 SE
Because molecules containing unexpected isotopes--carbon-13 in place of carbon-12 in carbon dioxide, say--weigh slightly more or less than their more common counterparts and yield valuable information about chemical processes, researchers are weighing molecules on ever ____________ scales.Because molecules containing unexpected isotopes--carbon-13 in place of carbon-12 in carbon dioxide, say--weigh slightly more or less than their more common counterparts and yield valuable information about chemical processes, researchers are weighing molecules on ever ____________ scales.
答案：B, E　finer; more sensitive
yield
yield: standard unit headword
#293 SE
We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not __________ repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not __________ repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.
答案：A, D　spurned; rebuffed
project
project: standard unit headword
#294 TC TRIPLE
Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded
resonate
resonate: standard unit headword
#295 SE
Recent discoveries suggest that mathematical techniques developed by ancient Babylonian astronomers ____________ those of medieval European scholars: Babylonian trapezoid procedures can be viewed as a concrete example of graphical methods devised by fourteenth-century philosopher Nicole Oresme.Recent discoveries suggest that mathematical techniques developed by ancient Babylonian astronomers ____________ those of medieval European scholars: Babylonian trapezoid procedures can be viewed as a concrete example of graphical methods devised by fourteenth-century philosopher Nicole Oresme.
答案：D, E　anticipate; prefigure
concrete
concrete: standard unit headword
#296 TC TRIPLE
The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading
contract
contract: standard unit headword
#297 SE
With representatives on every continent except Antarctica, bats are extraordinarily ____________, accounting for one in every five species of mammals alive today.With representatives on every continent except Antarctica, bats are extraordinarily ____________, accounting for one in every five species of mammals alive today.
答案：B, F　ubiquitous; widespread
mammal
mammal: standard unit headword
#298 TC TRIPLE
Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder
recount
recount: standard unit headword
#299 SE
The open star was so __________ in her last role--a shy, spurned lover who never gets over her youthful passion--that you begin to suspect some deep personal connection to the part.The open star was so __________ in her last role--a shy, spurned lover who never gets over her youthful passion--that you begin to suspect some deep personal connection to the part.
答案：A, D　affecting; moving
shy
shy: standard unit headword
#300 TC TRIPLE
In recent years in India, the works of such figures as Mohandas Gandhi, Vallabhbhai Patel, Subhas Chandra Bose, and Rabindranath Tagore have all benefited from (i) __________ interpretations of each man's life and thought. Jawaharlal Nehru, by contrast, has been subjected to (ii) __________ that border on caricature, portrayed as (iii) __________ a one-dimensional view of science and a vacuous universalism.In recent years in India, the works of such figures as Mohandas Gandhi, Vallabhbhai Patel, Subhas Chandra Bose, and Rabindranath Tagore have all benefited from (i) __________ interpretations of each man's life and thought. Jawaharlal Nehru, by contrast, has been subjected to (ii) __________ that border on caricature, portrayed as (iii) __________ a one-dimensional view of science and a vacuous universalism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　more nuanced; simplifications; a mouthpiece for
vacuous
vacuous: standard unit headword
#301 SE
While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.
答案：A, C　parochial; narrow
realize
realize: standard unit headword
#302 TC TRIPLE
Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　synonymous with; free from; appropriated by
realize
realize: standard unit headword
#303 SE
His lackadaisical approach to this project belies his well-known reputation for __________.His lackadaisical approach to this project belies his well-known reputation for __________.
答案：B, C　assiduousness; industry
project
project: standard unit headword
#304 TC TRIPLE
For any art lover who has sat through a high-stakes auction, where artworks often are applauded for their (i) __________, there is something undeniably (ii) __________ about encouraging more people to see (iii) __________, rather than dollar signs, when they look at paintings on museum walls.For any art lover who has sat through a high-stakes auction, where artworks often are applauded for their (i) __________, there is something undeniably (ii) __________ about encouraging more people to see (iii) __________, rather than dollar signs, when they look at paintings on museum walls.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　record-breaking prices; refreshing; creativity
auction
auction: standard unit headword
#305 SE
How much corruption exists in internationally funded development projects is a matter of __________, though one well-supported estimate is that it might affect 40 percent or more of such projects.How much corruption exists in internationally funded development projects is a matter of __________, though one well-supported estimate is that it might affect 40 percent or more of such projects.
答案：C, E　speculation; conjecture
project
project: standard unit headword
#306 TC TRIPLE
Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　acuity; social and cultural context; outstrip
invaluable
invaluable: standard unit headword
#307 SE
Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.
答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian
yield
yield: standard unit headword
#308 TC TRIPLE
Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　uncomfortable with; nefarious; an invasion of
intimate
intimate: standard unit headword
#309 SE
Unlike Chopin, whose etudes are musically sophisticated and adventurous, Czerny conceived his exercises as __________ works.Unlike Chopin, whose etudes are musically sophisticated and adventurous, Czerny conceived his exercises as __________ works.
答案：B, E　functional; utilitarian
conceive
conceive: standard unit headword
#310 TC TRIPLE
It has been suggested that the use of metaphors from the realms of battle and sports to describe arguments contributes to the (i) __________ of the argumentative process. Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.It has been suggested that the use of metaphors from the realms of battle and sports to describe arguments contributes to the (i) __________ of the argumentative process. Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　breakdown; promote; undermining
conception
conception: standard unit headword
#311 SE
Looking back on a project that they had approached with both great __________ and considerable aspirations, they were amused to recognize that neither the fears nor the hopes had been at all realistic.Looking back on a project that they had approached with both great __________ and considerable aspirations, they were amused to recognize that neither the fears nor the hopes had been at all realistic.
答案：D, E　trepidation; misgivings

During the past decade, there has been a quiet (i) __________ research into symbioses involving microorganisms: purely descriptive and speculative work has given way to investigations generating data that allow for (ii) __________ interpretation and thus (iii) __________ unfounded speculation.During the past decade, there has been a quiet (i) __________ research into symbioses involving microorganisms: purely descriptive and speculative work has given way to investigations generating data that allow for (ii) __________ interpretation and thus (iii) __________ unfounded speculation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　change in; defensible; reduce the tendency toward

The news that compatibility issues with the electronic device had been resolved was received with __________ by analysts and consumers, since they assumed that newer technologies would soon make the device obsolete.The news that compatibility issues with the electronic device had been resolved was received with __________ by analysts and consumers, since they assumed that newer technologies would soon make the device obsolete.
答案：A, B　indifference; apathy

Once photographs depicting important family events such as vacations or weddings are assembled and presented in a family album, the collection of photographs is treated as (i) __________ record of events; but what we (ii) __________ to (iii) __________ is the active selection process that led to the making of the album.Once photographs depicting important family events such as vacations or weddings are assembled and presented in a family album, the collection of photographs is treated as (i) __________ record of events; but what we (ii) __________ to (iii) __________ is the active selection process that led to the making of the album.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　an objective; tend; overlook

Although automated agricultural technology boosts yields and profits, its high degree of prescriptiveness diminishes the role of individual farmers by making their __________ almost irrelevant.Although automated agricultural technology boosts yields and profits, its high degree of prescriptiveness diminishes the role of individual farmers by making their __________ almost irrelevant.
答案：C, E　judgment; discretion
yield
yield: standard unit headword
#316 TC TRIPLE
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
confused
confused: standard unit headword
#317 SE
Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.
答案：B, F　multifarious; protean

Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)______, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：A, F　parlous; risky
regrettableroutine

#320 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)_______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)_______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)_______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)_______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event
trace
trace: standard unit headword
#321 TC TRIPLE
The (i)______ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)______ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)______ leader.The (i)______ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)______ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)______ leader.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　platitudinous; innocuous; conciliatory

答案：B, F　canned; formulaic

#323 TC TRIPLE
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
replicate
replicate: standard unit headword

Although initially it may be difficult to discern the essay’s message of peace and conciliation, a close reading reveals its essentially ______ nature.Although initially it may be difficult to discern the essay’s message of peace and conciliation, a close reading reveals its essentially ______ nature.
答案：A, F　pacific; dovish

#325 TC TRIPLE
China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)______; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many of its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)______. This human pressure has (iii)______ the South China tiger.China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)______; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many of its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)______. This human pressure has (iii)______ the South China tiger.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　stabilization; conundrum; forestall
bypass
bypass: standard unit headword
#326 SE
As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty

#327 TC TRIPLE
Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive
jettison

答案：C, F　insufficient; meager

Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace
confused

#332 SE

答案：D, E　checking; stemming

#333 TC TRIPLE
The research on otters' environmental requirements is surprisingly (i)______. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. Doing so may be (ii)______ in some kinds of terrain, such as Shetland where the Eurasian otters are active in daytime and have clear individual markings. There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. However, the field conditions are (iii)______.The research on otters' environmental requirements is surprisingly (i)______. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. Doing so may be (ii)______ in some kinds of terrain, such as Shetland where the Eurasian otters are active in daytime and have clear individual markings. There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. However, the field conditions are (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　difficult; relatively simple; exceptional
routine
routine: standard unit headword
#334 SE
Given that the department director was such a feeble contributor, sitting silently at important policy meetings and usually deferring to low-level aides, it was surprising that he had such a reputation for ______.Given that the department director was such a feeble contributor, sitting silently at important policy meetings and usually deferring to low-level aides, it was surprising that he had such a reputation for ______.
答案：B, F　perspicacity; discernment
magnanimity

#335 TC TRIPLE
Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)______. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)______. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains—even the air we breathe—seem the norm and therefore (iii)______.Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)______. 答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　absorbed; spurs; substitutes
incentive
incentive: standard unit headword
#381 SE
Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i) for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii) enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecule thick.Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i) for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii) enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecule thick.
答案：C, E　a significant struggle; entails

In 1960, journalist Murray Kempton argued that sociologists, seeking to (i) __________ of meaningful research findings, used (ii) __________ language to (iii) __________ the fraudulence of their discipline's claims to scientific status.In 1960, journalist Murray Kempton argued that sociologists, seeking to (i) __________ of meaningful research findings, used (ii) __________ language to (iii) __________ the fraudulence of their discipline's claims to scientific status.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　obscure the paucity; recondite; disguise
prestige
prestige: standard unit headword
#383 SE

Their forms thus necessarily become (i) over time and their original features are (ii) . GEARS: KBEINGRE)Ancient cart ruts found on Malta were created in soft limestone that begins to dissolve when exposed to rainfall. Their forms thus necessarily become (i) over time and their original features are (ii) . GEARS: KBEINGRE)
答案：B, F　degraded; obscured

#384 TC TRIPLE
Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　difficulty of; still more acute; specious
incentive
incentive: standard unit headword
#385 SE
Recently the novelist has (i) the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii) narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to runRecently the novelist has (i) the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii) narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to run
答案：B, D　forsaken; conventional

replicate: standard unit headword
#386 TC TRIPLE
Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to
deviate
deviate: standard unit headword
#387 SE
Motorcycle riders in London have discovered an ingenious, albeit___—_—s way off parking illegally without getting a ticket. [MUSARS: SkHEIPGRE)Motorcycle riders in London have discovered an ingenious, albeit___—_—s way off parking illegally without getting a ticket. [MUSARS: SkHEIPGRE)
答案：B, D　unscrupulous; dishonest
shrewd
shrewd: standard unit headword
#388 TC TRIPLE
Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty
perceptive
perceptive: standard unit headword
#389 SE
The scholar defended W.E.B. Du Bois from the charge of being ___—_ by pointing out that whenever Du Bois changed his views, he acknowledged it, explained his reasons for doing so, and moved on. [HM{SARS: sk ElMGRE)The scholar defended W.E.B. Du Bois from the charge of being ___—_ by pointing out that whenever Du Bois changed his views, he acknowledged it, explained his reasons for doing so, and moved on. [HM{SARS: sk ElMGRE)
答案：A, C　inconsistent; erratic

pugnacious: standard unit headword
#390 TC TRIPLE
A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　speculative; keep it up; gratuitous
sentimental
sentimental: standard unit headword
#391 SE
Though the play crackles with humor, the dialogue is less (i) when it comes to the drama’s emotional core. There the players tend to spell out their emotions in (ii) aphorisms, and repeat them as necessary.Though the play crackles with humor, the dialogue is less (i) when it comes to the drama’s emotional core. There the players tend to spell out their emotions in (ii) aphorisms, and repeat them as necessary.
答案：A, E　adroit; jejune

shrewd: standard unit headword
#392 TC TRIPLE
Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　tenacious; collapse; flourish
coalesce
coalesce: standard unit headword
#393 SE
Though the art of writing was well established in Egypt and Mesopotamia long before the Shang period, the Chinese writing that appeared then seems to have been an entirely__— development. [#MAARS: 5k#ZIMGRE)Though the art of writing was well established in Egypt and Mesopotamia long before the Shang period, the Chinese writing that appeared then seems to have been an entirely__— development. [#MAARS: 5k#ZIMGRE)
答案：C, F　homegrown; indigenous

routine: standard unit headword
#394 TC TRIPLE
In ancient China, tin-rich ores were not as (i) __________ as copper-rich ores, and bronze--an alloy of copper and tin--was (ii) __________ material, as tin frequently had to be transported over large distances. As a result, unalloyed copper was probably used whenever (iii) __________, since the metals used for farming and domestic purposes did not need to be as strong as the metals used for hunting and fighting.In ancient China, tin-rich ores were not as (i) __________ as copper-rich ores, and bronze--an alloy of copper and tin--was (ii) __________ material, as tin frequently had to be transported over large distances. As a result, unalloyed copper was probably used whenever (iii) __________, since the metals used for farming and domestic purposes did not need to be as strong as the metals used for hunting and fighting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　abundant; an expensive; practical
obsolete
obsolete: standard unit headword
#395 SE
The widespread (i) that has accompanied economic growth is not the result of the people’s adopting the leader’s own standards. Her personal (ii) is recognized by all.The widespread (i) that has accompanied economic growth is not the result of the people’s adopting the leader’s own standards. Her personal (ii) is recognized by all.
答案：B, E　corruption; integrity
conformity
conformity: standard unit headword
#396 TC TRIPLE
Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　ineptitudes; thoroughgoing; incompetent
profound
profound: standard unit headword
#397 SE
A Sociological jurisprudence has a (i) rather than (ii) focus: it is concerned with what actually goes on in the legal system, not with what ought to go on.A Sociological jurisprudence has a (i) rather than (ii) focus: it is concerned with what actually goes on in the legal system, not with what ought to go on.
答案：B, E　descriptive; a normative
humanitarian
humanitarian: standard unit headword
#398 TC TRIPLE
Failure to investigate (i) __________ a proposed new feature is desired by potential consumers of a product can lead to expensive mistakes: money is invested to incorporate the feature, whose value in the marketplace then turns out to be (ii) __________. Yet equally, relying on what consumers say can lead to potentially profitable innovations being (iii) __________. Consumers do not necessarily at once recognize how a new feature will expand the potential of an apparently familiar product.Failure to investigate (i) __________ a proposed new feature is desired by potential consumers of a product can lead to expensive mistakes: money is invested to incorporate the feature, whose value in the marketplace then turns out to be (ii) __________. Yet equally, relying on what consumers say can lead to potentially profitable innovations being (iii) __________. Consumers do not necessarily at once recognize how a new feature will expand the potential of an apparently familiar product.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　how much; trifling; overlooked
conceive
conceive: standard unit headword

Stigler brings a fundamental (i) to his protagonists, somehow treating them with respect even while he reveals these sometimes prickly characters behaving in (ii) ways.Stigler brings a fundamental (i) to his protagonists, somehow treating them with respect even while he reveals these sometimes prickly characters behaving in (ii) ways.
答案：B, F　sympathy; petulant

Their physiology (iii) their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.Researchers claim that early mammals evolved a multistep process to synthesize vitamin C in their own bodies but that tens of millions of years ago some primates became so (i) eating fruits rich in this vitamin that making their own became (ii) . Their physiology (iii) their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　accustomed to; an unnecessary cost; adapted to

答案：D, F　a difficult; an enigmatic

Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) __________; they were widely disparaged for being so (ii) __________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) __________.Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) __________; they were widely disparaged for being so (ii) __________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：A, D　attraction; benefit

#404 SE

答案：D, E　supplant; supersede

replicate: standard unit headword
#405 SE
The Chippewa regarded their treaties with the federal government as solemn covenants and therefore, throughout the twentieth century, met efforts by governmental officials to __________ those treaties with both open and hidden resistance.The Chippewa regarded their treaties with the federal government as solemn covenants and therefore, throughout the twentieth century, met efforts by governmental officials to __________ those treaties with both open and hidden resistance.
答案：B, E　abrogate; nullify
replicate
replicate: standard unit headword
#406 SE
The danger of isolation appears often in Anishinaabe stories but is nowhere more __________ than in the Cree and Ojibwa tales about the Windigo, a terrifying ice monster that is literally a lost soul.The danger of isolation appears often in Anishinaabe stories but is nowhere more __________ than in the Cree and Ojibwa tales about the Windigo, a terrifying ice monster that is literally a lost soul.
答案：B, D　striking; pronounced

#407 SE
Many types of rocks contain small voids that __________ vibrations: the greater the proportion of a rock's volume that is void, the more gradually vibrations travel through it.Many types of rocks contain small voids that __________ vibrations: the greater the proportion of a rock's volume that is void, the more gradually vibrations travel through it.
答案：B, D　dampen; impede
generate
generate: standard unit headword
#408 SE
Neuroscientists are discovering that our sensory systems are much more interconnected than previously thought: even in ordinary circumstances, our senses can __________ one another in extraordinary ways.Neuroscientists are discovering that our sensory systems are much more interconnected than previously thought: even in ordinary circumstances, our senses can __________ one another in extraordinary ways.
答案：C, F　abet; support

bypass: standard unit headword
#409 SE

答案：C, E　mercurial; volatile
pugnacious
pugnacious: standard unit headword
#410 SE
Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.
答案：C, D　insouciance; detachment

melancholy: standard unit headword
#411 SE
Duke Ellington managed his career with extreme deliberateness: nothing was __________, and even apparent compromises or trade-offs were actually artful accommodations leading to eventual triumphs.Duke Ellington managed his career with extreme deliberateness: nothing was __________, and even apparent compromises or trade-offs were actually artful accommodations leading to eventual triumphs.
答案：B, D　fortuitous; accidental

#412 SE

答案：A, E　insecure; precarious

#413 SE
Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.
答案：B, D　noticeable; conspicuous
